behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE: behavior text says "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels," which depends on real model diarization quality. Adjacent lower (System) miss: a real HTTP STT call with a fixed canned response can still pass while the model intermittently drops/mixes speaker labels on real meeting audio; this is specifically an output-quality failure, not generic module wiring. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because this behavior is about one model decision boundary (STT diarization quality), not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the production STT model and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels for known speaker turns used later for attendee attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS check: this behavior is parser logic (extract assignee + task text) and can fail entirely within one function/object boundary with no required I/O or cross-module handoff. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because the behavior text focuses on extraction logic itself, not module-boundary propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript lines into the task-assignment parser function and assert normalized output objects contain assignee and task text fields for explicit assignments.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE: behavior text says "live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee," which depends on model output completeness and attribution quality. Adjacent lower (System) miss: an end-to-end run with mocked/fixed summary text can always satisfy coverage even when the real LLM omits an attendee’s action item; this is not just wiring because failure comes from model judgment/content. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because this assertion can be evaluated on a single summarization decision from transcript input.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript inputs with known per-attendee assignments, call the real summarization model, and verify each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE: behavior text says "routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema," indicating a handoff between decision-identification output and schema-mapping module. Adjacent lower (Unit) miss: unit-testing only the mapper with idealized decision objects can pass while real extractor output shape/flags cause router fallback to narrative fields; this is a concrete module-boundary mismatch, not generic wiring hand-waving. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement, no dependence on real model output quality if "identified decisions" are provided deterministically, and no multi-agentic chain required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because text scopes the behavior to routing of already identified decisions, not correctness of model identification.
behaviors.test_description: Wire extractor output fixtures through the real routing/serialization modules and assert decision items are emitted under `decisions` schema keys and excluded from narrative-only fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE: behavior text says "key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section," which tests model extraction/placement quality from natural language. Adjacent lower (System) miss: a deterministic stubbed summary can place decisions correctly every time and hide real-model failures where decisions are paraphrased into narrative only; this is model output content quality, not just transport/integration. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because the behavior can be validated on a single summarization pass rather than multiple dependent agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts containing explicit decision statements through the real summarizer and assert those decisions are present in the structured `decisions` section and absent as narrative-only mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE: behavior text explicitly requires "splits into segments," "processes them sequentially," and "merging the results into a single coherent summary"—multiple dependent agentic operations across segments. Adjacent lower (Agentic) miss: a single-call agentic test cannot expose cross-segment ordering, carryover, and merge-coherence failures that only appear after several segment-level model calls are chained. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because even with real infra, a non-agentic or single-step test cannot validate multi-step model-dependent coherence after merge.
behaviors.test_description: Use a >60-minute real or production-like recording, execute true segmenting + sequential STT/summarization + merge pipeline, and validate final summary coherence and complete structured sections across segment boundaries.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: PROOF-BUNDLE: behavior text says "full pipeline" with "upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output" on a "real meeting recording," which is an end-to-end chain including multiple model-dependent stages. Adjacent lower (Agentic) miss: testing only one model step (or even one real model call) cannot catch failures in cross-stage orchestration, state transfer, and unattended completion across upload→STT→LLM→delivery. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because the requirement includes sequential agentic stages, not just infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end job on a real meeting file through actual upload, transcription, summarization, and delivery path; assert successful completion and valid structured output without human retries/intervention.
